items.title: Xiaomi 开源 MiMo-V2.5-Pro (1.02T MoE, 强 Agent 编码)
items.summary: 4 月 28 日小米开源 MiMo-V2.5 系列(MIT)。Pro 版 1.02T/42B 激活,混合注意力(128-token 滑窗 : 全局 = 6:1,KV cache ~7×↓),1M 上下文。配 MiMo Code agent harness,厂商基准在 SWE-bench Verified(82 vs 79)/Pro(62 vs 55)/Terminal-Bench 2(73 vs 69)超 Claude Code+Sonnet 4.6。尚无 Ascend 移植。数字均厂商口径,provisional。

items.title: Seed 2.1 Pro 抢先版上线 LMArena (闭源, 编码/Agent)
items.summary: 6 月 19 日 ByteDance 把 Seed-2.1-Pro-Preview 放上 Code Arena 试用:前端编码子榜 1539 Elo、全球第 8,约等于 Claude Opus 4.6。闭源 API、无 Ascend 移植——是标杆而非 NPU 载荷。完整基准随正式版未来几周公布。分析见 Blog → Dispatch 01。

items.title: 新一批开源模型 (6月中): Qwen3 Coder Next · MiniMax M2.7 · Nemotron 3 Ultra
items.summary: 6 月中又一波开放权重发布:Qwen3 Coder Next、MiniMax M2.5/M2.7 Highspeed、NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra 等(新闻聚合,基准/细节待官方)。

items.title: DeepSeek-V4 & GLM-5 run on Ascend 910B (vLLM-Ascend)
items.summary: vLLM-Ascend's 2026 support matrix adds DeepSeek-V4 (custom 910B ops, MTP KV-cache sharding for spec decoding) and GLM-5 (W8A8C8 quant); Qwen3.5 + W8A8 across Qwen3/DeepSeek/MiniMax. MiniMax-M3 not yet listed.

items.title: GLM-5.2 open-sourced (MIT, 1M context)
items.summary: Launched Jun 13 2026; MIT open weights now live on Hugging Face (zai-org/GLM-5.2). 753B params (GLM-5.1-class 744B MoE / 40B active), context 200K→1M, max output 131K, coding-first. No official benchmarks at launch; media cites it edging GPT-5.5 on long-horizon coding (FrontierSWE/SWE-Marathon) at ~1/6 cost — treat as provisional.
items.ascendNote: GLM-5 is supported on vLLM-Ascend (W8A8C8 quant); GLM-5.2 not yet listed by name.

items.title: MiniMax-M3 with MSA sparse attention
items.summary: Open-weight 1M-context model, native multimodal + agentic coding. MSA (blockwise sparse on GQA) cuts attention compute ~28.4x at 1M context. Official report on arXiv.
items.ascendNote: MiniMax family has W8A8/QuaRot on vLLM-Ascend (M2.x); M3 not yet listed by name.

items.title: DeepSeek-V4 (1.6T MoE, 1M context)
items.summary: V4-Pro/Flash with hybrid sparse attention (CSA+HCA), Multi-Token Prediction, and a multi-teacher distillation pipeline over 32T+ tokens. Official tech report PDF on Hugging Face.
items.ascendNote: Supported on Ascend 910B via vLLM-Ascend (custom ops, MTP KV-cache sharding for speculative decoding).

items.title: Agentic RL: a distinct paradigm (survey)
items.summary: Survey reframing agentic RL vs single-turn RLVR — multi-turn credit assignment, tool/environment interaction, long-horizon planning.

items.title: Stable Asynchrony: variance-controlled off-policy RL
items.summary: Controls heavy-tailed importance weights from stale rollouts to keep async/off-policy GRPO stable at high asynchrony.

items.title: Ascend 950PR / 950DT announced (FP8, self-built HBM)
items.summary: Next-gen Ascend with native FP8/MXFP4 and Huawei's own HBM — 950PR (inference) ~Q1 2026, 950DT (training) ~Q4 2026. Opens the door to FP8 RL on Ascend.

items.title: vLLM-Ascend v0.13.0 released
items.summary: Latest Ascend rollout backend — broader model coverage and performance; the rollout engine RL stacks target on NPU.

items.title: DeepSeek-V3.2 + Sparse Attention (DSA)
items.summary: Open-weight frontier model with trainable sparse attention for cheaper long-context; RL post-training continues the R1 lineage.

items.title: GDRO-RL: distributionally robust GRPO
items.summary: Rollout-/Prompt-GDRO reweight toward worst-case prompt distributions on top of GRPO for more robust reasoning RL.

items.title: MindSpeed-RL: Distributed RL Post-Training on Ascend NPU
items.summary: Native distributed GRPO-family RL on Ascend, with experiments up to DeepSeek-R1-MoE-671B on a 384-NPU superpod — the strongest large-scale RL-on-Ascend evidence to date.

items.title: AsyncFlow: Asynchronous Streaming RL Post-Training
items.summary: An async streaming RL framework targeting the throughput limits of synchronous/colocated RL — directly relevant to NPU constraints like the missing vLLM sleep mode.

items.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Pure large-scale RL (GRPO + verifiable rewards) elicits emergent reasoning with no SFT (R1-Zero); catalyzed the 2025 RLVR wave.

items.title: GSPO: Group Sequence Policy Optimization
items.summary: Sequence-level importance ratios and clipping fix GRPO instability and notably stabilize MoE RL; contributed to Qwen3.

items.title: DAPO: An Open-Source LLM RL System at Scale
items.summary: GRPO + Clip-Higher, dynamic sampling, token-level loss, and overlong reward shaping; 50 on AIME 2024 with Qwen2.5-32B at half the steps.

items.title: ProRL: Prolonged RL Expands Reasoning Boundaries
items.summary: Stabilizes very long RL runs (KL control, reference resetting) and shows prolonged RL uncovers genuinely new reasoning strategies.

items.title: MiniMax-M1: Test-Time Compute with Lightning Attention (CISPO)
items.summary: Open hybrid-attention reasoner trained with CISPO (clips IS-weights, not tokens); full RL on 512 H800s in ~3 weeks.

items.title: AReaL: Large-Scale Asynchronous RL for Language Reasoning
items.summary: Fully decouples rollout from training with staleness-enhanced PPO for ~2.77x speedup; now ships a stable Ascend branch.

items.title: Native Sparse Attention (NSA)
items.summary: Trainable, hardware-aligned hierarchical sparse attention matching full attention with large speedups at 64K length.

items.title: The Entropy Mechanism of RL for Reasoning LLMs
items.summary: Explains policy-entropy collapse via log-prob/advantage covariance and proposes Clip-Cov / KL-Cov to sustain exploration.

items.title: Pretraining LLMs with NVFP4
items.summary: Large-scale 4-bit (NVFP4) pretraining of a 12B model on 10T tokens matching FP8 — the most ambitious FP4 training run reported.

items.title: GLM-4.5: Agentic, Reasoning, and Coding Foundation Models
items.summary: Hybrid thinking/direct MoE (355B/32B) trained with a difficulty-based RL curriculum on the open-source 'slime' framework.

items.title: CompactionRL: Reinforcement Learning with Context Compaction for Long-Horizon Agents
items.summary: Long-horizon agentic LLMs are increasingly limited by finite context windows, as extended interaction trajectories can exceed the maximum context length before a task is completed. Context compaction offers a natural solution by summarizing previous interaction states and continuing the rollout unde…

items.title: TimeThink: Reasoning with Time for Video LLMs
items.summary: Video reasoning requires models to identify and verify temporally localized evidence within long video sequences. Recent Video Large Language Models (Video-LLMs) have shown promising reasoning abilities when aligned with reinforcement learning, yet existing approaches typically rely on outcome-based…

items.title: STAPO: Selective Trajectory-Aware Policy Optimization for LLM Agent Training
items.summary: Reinforcement Learning (RL) is the dominant paradigm for training Large Language Model (LLM) agents on long-horizon tasks. However, sparse and delayed rewards often lead to trajectory neglect, in which agents lose focus on the task goal and interaction history at intermediate steps. Prior work has e…

items.title: CARL: Constraint-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Planning with LLMs
items.summary: Despite their strong reasoning capabilities and extensive world knowledge, Large Language Models (LLMs) frequently generate plans that violate task constraints, undermining their reliability in real-world applications. This deficiency arises from a lack of systematic mechanisms to incorporate constr…

items.title: Turning Off-Policy Tokens On-Policy: A Plug-in Approach for Improving LLM Alignment
items.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) post-training for large language models (LLMs) follows a efficient paradigm of "rollout then update", which inevitably results in off-policy training data. To resolve this, Importance sampling (IS) is proposed, while the token-level ratios compound over long sequences, ca…

items.title: RSPO: Reward-Swap Policy Optimization for Multi-Turn LLM Agents
items.summary: Reinforcement learning holds significant potential for training large language models (LLMs) to handle multi-turn interactive tasks. However, in long-horizon, multi-turn tasks characterized by sparse outcome rewards, directly training with outcome rewards often results in slow convergence due to the…

items.title: Beyond the Need for Speed: Energy-Aware Code Generation via Simulation-Guided Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Code models strictly prioritize functional correctness, leaving software energy efficiency as an unoptimized byproduct. Training models to generate energy-efficient code requires reproducible feedback at scale, which physical hardware measurement cannot reliably provide due to variance.   In this pa…

items.title: Weak-to-Strong Generalization via Direct On-Policy Distillation
items.summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) is a powerful recipe for improving language-model reasoning, but it is expensive to repeat on every new strong model because the target model must generate many rollouts during training. As models scale, post-training itself becomes a bottleneck.…

items.title: Do Vision-Language-Action Models Mean What They Say? On the Role of Faithfulness in Embodied Reasoning
items.summary: Embodied Chain-of-Thought has emerged as a promising mechanism to enhance robot decision-making and interpretability in black-box Vision-Language Action (VLA) models. However, whether this verbalized Chain-of-Thought truthfully reflects the policy's underlying decision process remains poorly underst…

items.title: Adaptive Inference Batching using Policy Gradients
items.summary: Inference serving systems must balance throughput and latency under bursty, heterogeneous workloads, yet the industry standard remains static batching policies that require manual tuning and cannot adapt to shifting traffic. We investigate whether reinforcement learning (RL) can learn adaptive batch…

items.title: Optimal Base Station Placement for Beyond 5G Networks with Non-Convex Topology
items.summary: This paper investigates the optimal placement of a millimeter-wave (mmWave) base station (BS) within a realistic U-shaped environment with non-convex topology. The problem is challenging and NP-hard due to the non-convex topology and the non-convex objective functions which are the sum-rate maximiza…

items.title: When Claws Remember but Do Not Tell: Stealthy Memory Injection in Persistent Personal Agents
items.summary: Persistent personal agents combine long-term memory with access to users' external environments, enabling personalized foreground assistance and proactive background execution. This integration also creates a new path to compromise: untrusted external content can be silently written into persistent …

items.title: Relational Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Pricing in High-Speed Railway Markets
items.summary: In liberalised railway systems, operators must set prices dynamically in an environment with partial observability, as they retain private information about their objectives and performance, where regulatory constraints prohibit communication or direct information exchange between competitors to pre…

items.title: RL-Ballast: Ship Ballast Water Path Planning and Clog Prediction via Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Under the Shipping 4.0 paradigm, autonomous and reduced-crew vessels require intelligent internal systems to maintain operational safety and structural stability. Ballast-water control is essential for ship trim and integrity, but conventional rule-based or manual approaches have limited adaptabilit…

items.title: Integrated Altruistic and Fairness Preference Induces Advanced Mutual Cooperation in Sequential Social Dilemmas
items.summary: Inducing cooperation among distributed agents is still a difficult problem in the field of multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), particularly in social dilemma situations. There, individual interests are misaligned with the common good and individual rationality leads to suboptimal group outcom…

items.title: Listen, Think, Transcribe: Continuous Latent Test-Time Scaling for ASR
items.summary: End-to-end ASR models transcribe in a single pass, leaving no room for the decoder to revisit hard inputs. We propose LatentASR, a parameter-efficient method that adds continuous latent test-time scaling to a frozen ASR backbone. Two small trainable modules drive it: a Latent Adapter that iterativel…

items.title: Unified Audio Intelligence Without Regressing on Text Intelligence
items.summary: Audio intelligence involves understanding, reasoning about, and generating both audio and speech. In this work, we introduce Nemotron-Labs-Audex-30B-A3B (Audex), a unified audio-text LLM built on Nemotron-Cascade-2-30B-A3B, a strong text-only MoE LLM. Audex adopts a simple unified design with a sing…

items.title: When Words Predict Workload
items.summary: Standard distributed \ac{llm} schedulers rely on static token counts or rolling latency averages, making them susceptible to failures on statutorily constrained text. On \ac{epo} claims governed by Article 84 \ac{epc}, linguistic rigidity makes human and machine authorship statistically indistinguis…

items.title: HunyuanOCR-1.5: Making Lightweight OCR VLMs Faster and Better
items.summary: We present HunyuanOCR-1.5, a lightweight end-to-end OCR-specialized vision-language model. HunyuanOCR unifies document parsing, text spotting, information extraction, text-image translation, and multi-image document understanding within a single end-to-end VLM. Building upon the lightweight architec…

items.title: MoP-JEPA: Hard-Assigned Predictor Mixtures for Stochastic JEPA World Models
items.summary: JEPA world models predict the next latent state with a single deterministic predictor trained by latent regression. We show that this fails structurally when the environment is stochastic: at a branching transition, the regression-optimal predictor outputs the conditional mean of the successor embed…

items.title: Shift-MoE-Based DJSCC for CSI Feedback in Multi-User Pinching-Antenna Systems
items.summary: In frequency-division duplexing systems, the performance gains of pinching-antenna systems (PASS) critically depend on accurate channel state information (CSI) at the base station. However, PASS CSI exhibits structured correlations over the waveguide-antenna grid and pronounced heterogeneity across …

items.title: Regime-Conditional Stabilisation of LLM-Augmented Cooperative Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) offer a natural interface for translating human objectives into reward signals for cooperative multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), yet the training-time dynamics of this integration remain poorly understood. We show that dynamically updating LLM-generated reward w…

items.title: dOPSD: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Diffusion Language Models
items.summary: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) generate text by iteratively denoising a masked sequence, offering a parallel alternative to autoregressive models, but eliciting strong reasoning through post-training remains difficult: supervised fine-tuning is off-policy and suffers from exposure bias, whi…

items.title: LLM-as-a-Tutor: Policy-Aware Prompt Adaptation for Non-Verifiable RL
items.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) for non-verifiable instruction following increasingly relies on LLM judges with prompt-specific rubrics as reward signals. While recent methods adapt these rubrics to the evolving policy during training, the training prompts themselves remain static, drawn from fixed corp…

items.title: Auto-AEG: Scalable Data Construction for Open-Vocabulary Audio Event Grounding
items.summary: Large Audio-Language Models (LALMs) reason fluently about sound yet struggle to localize precisely when events occur, while classical Sound Event Detection attains frame-level precision only over a closed label set. At the intersection of these paradigms lies the task of Open-Vocabulary Audio Event …

items.title: Nemotron-Labs-3-Puzzle-75B-A9B: Compressing Hybrid MoE LLMs
items.summary: We present Nemotron-Labs-3-Puzzle-75B-A9B, a compressed variant of Nemotron-3-Super optimized for interactive deployment. We designed the model to maximize server throughput under high user throughput constraints. In interactive serving workloads on a single 8xB200 node, Puzzle-75B-A9B achieves appr…

items.title: RL Forgets! Towards Continual Policy Optimization
items.summary: Continual post-training is becoming a central paradigm for adapting vision-language models to evolving tasks. Recent work has increasingly favored reinforcement learning over supervised fine-tuning, driven by the belief that reinforcement learning is inherently less prone to forgetting. However, the…

items.title: On the effectiveness of reward functions in reinforcement learning for confidence calibration of large language models
items.summary: In this paper, we consider the setting where large language models (LLMs) are trained using reinforcement learning (RL) to simultaneously improve reasoning accuracy and verbalize its confidence. Our reward scheme uses two functions for rewarding confidence verbalized by the LLM: one when the LLM is …

items.title: CritiqueDriveVLM: From Verifier-Guided Reinforcement Learning to Latent Thought Distillation for Autonomous Driving
items.summary: End-to-end Vision-Language Models (VLMs) show immense potential in autonomous driving. However, standard Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) often suffers from reasoning hallucinations and conservative biases. While traditional tool-augmented frameworks and Chain-of-Thought (CoT) approaches mitigate these …

items.title: Causal ASCEND: Scalable Two-tier Causal Discovery on High Dimensional Multi-omics Data
items.summary: Biological systems exhibit a hierarchical structure, characterised by directed flow from upstream regulators to downstream effects. Although this ordering provides a natural scaffold for causal inference, most causal discovery and GRN methods either ignore the tiered organisation or condition on all…

items.title: HiFA4: Training-Free 4-bit FlashAttention on Ascend HIF4 NPUs for LLM Inference
items.summary: We present HiFA4, a post-training operator-level design that executes both QK^T and PV in FlashAttention as 4-bit HIF4 Cube GEMMs for LLM inference on Ascend NPUs, while maintaining the online softmax state in FP16. To our knowledge, HiFA4 is the first Ascend-HIF4-targeted design of this kind evalua…

items.title: AI-RAN on NPUs: Baseband Processing Without Baseband Chips
items.summary: AI-RAN aims to unify artificial intelligence and radio access network workloads on a shared compute substrate. While this paradigm has so far been demonstrated primarily on Graphics Processing Units (GPUs), it remains unclear whether Neural Processing Units (NPUs), which are AI accelerators optimize…

items.title: Quantize the Target, Quantize the Drafter: Efficient Inference with Qwen3.5-4B
items.summary: This report describes our approach to the Efficient Qwen Competition, where the goal is to enable low-latency serving of Qwen3.5-4B on a resource-constrained NVIDIA A10G GPU. Our system combines a quantized target model with speculative decoding. To recover accuracy, we apply quantization-aware dist…

items.title: Lyapunov-Guided Training for Hardware-Safe Neural Networks Under Fixed-Point Arithmetic
items.summary: Low-precision neural networks are attractive for resource-constrained hardware, but fixed-point arithmetic introduces failure modes that are often hidden by idealised quantisation models. In particular, two's-complement overflow wrapping can corrupt hidden activations by changing both their magnitud…

items.title: Full-Stack FP4: Stable LLM Pretraining with Quantized Projections, Optimizers, and Attention
items.summary: Recent NVFP4 pretraining methods mainly target transformer linear layers, leaving optimizer states, optimizer arithmetic and attention underexplored in 4-bit pipelines. This critical gap blocks stable full-stack 4-bit pretraining, as the three core modules exhibit unique numerical failure patterns: …

items.title: Two Black Boxes, One Solver: Encoder Probing and Decoder Attribution for Neural Multi-Attribute VRP under Hard-Mask and Recourse Decoders
items.summary: Neural autoregressive solvers for the Multi-Attribute Vehicle Routing Problem (MAVRP) reach competitive cost but offer no per-step justification, a problem when dispatchers must validate, accept, or compare them. We open two complementary black boxes in one protocol. On the encoder side, linear prob…

items.title: Optimal Mixture-of-Experts Model Averaging for Conditional Generative Models
items.summary: Conditional generative models have emerged as powerful tools for sampling from target conditional distributions, driving substantial advances across a wide range of scientific and applied domains. As these models proliferate, practitioners often face multiple plausible generators whose performance c…

items.title: WPG-MoE: Weak-Prior-Guided Dense Mixture-of-Experts for User-Level Social Media Depression Detection
items.summary: Online social media posts provide scalable signals for early depression screening, and recent studies mainly improve pre-classification evidence through risk-post selection, symptom grounding, and clinically informed feature construction. However, these screening-stage designs often leave final deci…

items.title: BrownoutMoE: Structure-Aware Expert Grouping for Efficient and Accurate LLM Web-based Services
items.summary: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in Web-facing services, where inference must be both accurate and responsive under bursty demand. Although MoE models improve parameter efficiency through sparse expert activation, efficient MoE inference remains challen…

items.title: ELiTeFormer: An Efficient Transformer for FPGAs
items.summary: Transformer blocks are prevalent in large language model (LLM) but present deployment challenges due to their challenging computational and memory demands. While prior work has typically optimized attention mechanisms or feed-forward networks (FFNs) separately, few hardware (HW) architecture have jo…

items.title: HyperParallel-Mpipe: A Composable Algebra System for Optimizing MLLM Training over Supernode Clusters
items.summary: Modern AI applications have expanded beyond text-only interaction into a wide range of multimodal scenarios, making multimodal large language models (MLLMs) crucial for both research and industry. However, compared with traditional decoder-only LLM training, large-scale MLLM training often shows muc…

items.title: SAF3R: Dynamic Sparse Attention for Feed-Forward 3D Reconstruction Transformers
items.summary: Feed-forward 3D reconstruction (F3R) transformers have recently achieved remarkable success. However, scaling them to long image sequences remains challenging, as the quadratic complexity of cross-view global attention quickly becomes the dominant computational bottleneck. While recent efforts attem…

items.title: Reading Between the Dots: Decoding Hidden Computation across Filler Tokens
items.summary: Frontier LLMs can perform multi-step reasoning over content-free filler tokens like dots or counting sequences, producing correct answers with no visible chain-of-thought (CoT). This is a limit case for behavioral oversight, where surface tokens carry no information about the underlying reasoning. B…

items.title: HyperVAttention: Efficient Sparse Attention with Spatio-Temporal Clustering for Video Diffusion
items.summary: Video Diffusion Transformers (VDiTs) have demonstrated significant capabilities in high-fidelity video generation. However, their ability to produce long-duration videos is fundamentally constrained by the quadratic complexity of the self-attention mechanism. Recent clustering-based sparse attention…

items.title: Hierarchical Sparse Attention Done Right: Toward Infinite Context Modeling
items.summary: Scaling modern large language models (LLMs) to long contexts is limited by the quadratic computation cost, and poor length extrapolation of dense attention. Chunk-wise sparse attention offers a promising alternative, but all existing methods fall short of full attention because of their inaccurate c…

items.title: Accelerating MRI Colon Volume Measurements and Reducing Inter-Observer Variation through Automatic Segmentation and Human-in-the-Loop Correction
items.summary: The movement distribution, and volume of both chyme and gas in the colon, are important metrics to understand colonic function in health, disease, and the effects of treatments and different foodstuffs. Current methods available for assessment of these colonic contents using MRI consist mainly of ma…

items.title: Distributionally Robust Listwise Preference Optimization
items.summary: Existing robust preference optimization for language-model alignment mainly studies pairwise supervision and places robustness at the dataset, prompt, or preference-pair level. We instead study listwise preference optimization under ranking-label uncertainty: given a prompt and a candidate list, the…

items.title: Prediction of Radiotherapy-Induced Hematologic Toxicity in Cervical Cancer with Cohort-Aware Framework
items.summary: Hematologic toxicity (HT) is a major dose-limiting complication of pelvic radiotherapy for cervical cancer. Although radiomic and dosiomic features improve HT prediction beyond dosimetric metrics, their performance is highly sensitive to contour variability, limiting generalizability. We developed a…

items.title: Hawk: Harnessing Hardware-Aware Knowledge for High-Performance NPU Kernel Generation
items.summary: Developing high-performance kernels for Neural Processing Units (NPUs) is a critical industry bottleneck, requiring developers to manually navigate implicit hardware constraints and strict memory hierarchies. While large language models offer immense automation potential, they fail catastrophically …

items.title: ReContext: Recursive Evidence Replay as LLM Harness for Long-Context Reasoning
items.summary: Understanding and reasoning over long contexts has become a key requirement for deploying large language models (LLMs) in realistic applications. Although recent LLMs support increasingly long context windows, they often fail to use relevant evidence that is already present in the input, revealing a…

items.title: MxGLUT: A Reconfigurable LUT-Centric Broadcast Dataflow Accelerator for Mixed-Precision GEMM
items.summary: Large language model (LLM) inference suffers from growing inefficiency across the prefill and decode phases, especially under weight-only quantization, where activations remain in FP8 while weights are compressed to low-bit integers. Existing LUT-based accelerators mainly target FP8-INT4 computation…

items.title: NPUsper: Eliminating Redundant Computation for Real-Time Whisper on Mobile NPUs
items.summary: We present NPUsper, a live transcription system that makes Whisper efficient on mobile NPUs by eliminating redundant computation. To avoid the heavy padding used by prior streaming systems, NPUsper detects hallucinated tokens online from temporal patterns in decoder cross-attention, allowing each in…

items.title: HBM Is Not All You Need: Efficient Disaggregated LLM Serving across Memory-heterogeneous Accelerators
items.summary: LLM inference comprises a compute-bound prefill phase and a memory-bound decode phase, and recent systems disaggregate them onto separate hardware. Yet today's datacenter GPUs rely on costly HBM whose bandwidth sits almost entirely idle during prefill. LLM serving across memory-heterogeneous acceler…

items.title: W4A4 Quantization for Inference on Wan2.2-I2V-A14B
items.summary: We summarize our submission to Sub-Challenge 1: W4A4 Quantization for Inference (HiF4 / MXFP4) of the ICME 2026 Low-Bit-width Large-Model Quantization Challenge. The sub-challenge targets 4-bit weight and 4-bit activation inference on Wan-AI/Wan2.2-I2V-A14B under HiF4 or MXFP4 numerical formats. We …

items.title: Mechanism-Driven Monitors for Preemptive Detection of LLM Training Instability
items.summary: Frontier large language model training consumes massive accelerator fleets and long wall-clock computation, making stability failures costly when they occur. After a numerical or a hyperparameter fault has already destabilized the training dynamics, it may continue for thousands of steps while loss …

items.title: SharQ: Bridging Activation Sparsity and FP4 Quantization for LLM Inference
items.summary: Low-bit floating-point formats and semi-structured sparsity are increasingly supported by modern accelerators, yet combining them for LLM activation compression remains challenging: activations contain input-dependent outliers that dominate block scales in FP4 quantization, and directly applying N:M…

items.title: HyperQuant: A Rate-Distortion-Optimal Quantization Pipeline for Large Language and Diffusion Models
items.summary: We present HyperQuant (Hadamard, optimallY Packing, Entropy Rice-coding), a unified post-training quantization pipeline for the weights and the KV cache of large language and diffusion transformers. Across a suite of self-contained experiments (Table 1), HyperQuant outperforms the recent HIGGS schem…
